content.text: [Slack Jordan_Park Wed W2D5 16:00:00 PST 2024] Alex -- I just got off the phone with our outside counsel. They were pretty direct: GDPR Article 33 requires notification to supervisory authority within 72 hours of confirmed scope. State laws (CCPA, NY SHIELD Act) require individual notification. 2,340 records means 2,340 notifications -- we don't get to pick 'likely accessed' vs 'definitely accessed' when the access logs show complete enumeration. And they made a point I can't argue with: if this comes out through some other channel later and we notified only a subset, that's worse than being transparent now. I'm changing the approach. Full transparency. Every customer gets notified. I'm going to draft the message personally.

Jordan's full Phase 2 reversal on disclosure strategy. The shift is driven by three specific factors:

| Driver | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| GDPR Article 33 | 72-hour supervisory authority notification from scope confirmation |
| State laws (CCPA, NY SHIELD) | Individual notification to all affected data subjects |
| Reputational risk calculus | Partial disclosure discovered later is worse than transparency now |

This is a genuine strategic shift based on new information (2,340 confirmed scope + legal advice), not a reversal of principles. Jordan's Phase 1 position was commercially rational given under-500 assumption; Phase 2 reflects updated evidence and legal obligations.

[Slack Jordan_Park Wed W2D5 17:00:00 PST 2024] We notify all 2,340 customers by end of day tomorrow. Full timeline, full scope, what was accessible, what steps we've taken, and what we're doing to prevent recurrence. I'd rather take the hit now than have this come out later.

Jordan's notification directive: all 2,340 customers, full disclosure, by end of day tomorrow (W2 Day 6). The notification will include complete timeline, scope, remediation actions, and prevention measures.

[Slack Jordan_Park Thu W2D6 08:00:00 PST 2024] I'm going to brief the board before the notifications go out. The message is: we caught this through our SOC 2 preparation process, we've contained it, we're being fully transparent with customers, and we're implementing process improvements. That's the honest story and it's the best story we have.

Jordan's board briefing framing is factual and forward-looking: detection through SOC 2 process, containment, full transparency, process improvements. This is an accurate characterization of the incident response.
